title: Pleiocarpidia bracteolata
taxonomy: Pleiocarpidia bracteolata is classified in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family is one of the largest and most diverse in the plant kingdom, with over 600 genera and 13,000 species. The genus Pleiocarpidia is a group of flowering plants native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. By understanding its classification, researchers can better comprehend the evolutionary relationships and ecological roles of this species within its native habitat.

intro: Pleiocarpidia bracteolata is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Rubiaceae family and is also known as "Napo leaf". The health benefits of Pleiocarpidia bracteolata include its use in treating various health conditions, such as fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents found in this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Pleiocarpidia bracteolata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms associated with colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
